hypothesis: Incorporating explicit energy consumption and purification-cost models into the economic-efficiency metric will produce a threshold energy-price at which the CaO+SiO2 synthesis route ceases to be cost-effective and alternative, higher-energy pathways (e.g., Ca3SiO5+SiO2 or Ca3SiO5+Si3N4) become the optimal synthesis choices for Ca2SiO4.
